calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion Of To Vacate Renewal Of Clerk Summary Judgment Of July 29, 2011
Off Calendar Sep-16-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Kirsten Galler, Esq. (213.765.1000; kirsten.galler@calbar.ca.gov) Assistant General Counsel for Plaintiff, The State Bar of California.
Douglas R. Green, Esq. (714.406.2000; DRGreengo1@juno.com) for Defendant, Mae R. Galvez-Lantion.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAE GALVEZ-LANTION'S Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Clerk Summary Judgment Of July 29, 2011.

Defendant's (MAE RATUNIL GALVEZ-LANTION) motion to vacate renewal of clerk summary judgment motion is denied.

Defendant initiated the original 2002 proceeding by affirmatively requesting resignation with charges pending. The 2011 Entry of Judgment was proper because the State Bar had ten years from the 3/18/02 order to request the entry. (CCP 337.5.) Further, on 1/26/21 Judge Schulman held that Defendant failed to show the 2011 judgment was void or that the Court lacked jurisdiction. Thus, the only outstanding issue is whether the amount of the renewed judgement was correct because it contains new accrued interest charges.

Here, the final judgment amount is correct as the State Bar is entitled to recover filing fees and post judgment interest. (CCP 685.010(a) ["Interest accrues at the rate of 10 percent per annum on the principal amount of a money judgment remaining unsatisfied."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SUZANNE C. GRANDT, Esq. of the State Bar Of California's Office Of General Counsel (Tel: 415-538-2388) for Plaintiff/Petitioner The State Bar of California;
Atty. DOUGLAS R. GREEN, Esq. (Tel: 714-406-2000) for Defendant/Respondent Mae R. Galvez Lantion.

Hearing Re: Motion Of Defendant/Respondent, Mae R. Galvez-Lantion To Vacate Clerk Of The Superior Court Summary Judgment Of July 29, 2011.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to vacate judgment is denied. Defendant fails to show that the judgment is void or that plaintiff or the Court acted in excess of its jurisdiction.

On March 18, 2002, Defendant Mae Ratunil Galvez-Lantion resigned with charges pending from the State Bar of California. The Supreme Court awarded costs in favor of the State Bar pursuant to Business and Professions Code section 6086.10 ("In any order . . . accepting a resignation with a disciplinary matter pending, the Supreme Court shall include a direction that the licensee shall pay costs"). Business and Professions Code section 6086.10(a) provides that such an order imposing costs is enforceable as a money judgment. That subsection further provides that "[t]he State Bar may collect these costs through any means provided by law." California Rules of Court 9.23(b) states that "[t]he State Bar may file a certified copy of a final disciplinary order assessing costs . . . along with a certified copy of the certificate of costs . . . .with the clerk of the superior court of any county. The clerk must immediately enter judgment in conformity with the order." The State Bar properly complied with this prescribed procedure. As in any other case, the State Bar is entitled to recover its filing fee pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5(a)(1) and post-judgment interest under Code of Civil Procedure section 685.010. Defendant's motion, brought more than nine years after entry of that judgment in July 2011, verges on the frivolous, and her intemperate diatribe attacking the State Bar and its counsel on reply is groundless.

Counsel for the State Bar is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.
